subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Weather Alerts Decoded
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Weather Alerts Decoded.

hook.text: Watch, warning, advisory—people confuse them constantly. The difference can save your life.

buildup.text: A watch means conditions are favorable—be prepared. A warning means it's happening—take action now.

discovery.text: An advisory falls between—conditions won't be severe but could cause inconvenience or minor danger.

twist.text: Wireless Emergency Alerts now push tornado warnings directly to cell phones—no app needed.

climax.text: Having a NOAA weather radio provides alerts even when cell towers are down.

punchline.text: Watch: prepare. Warning: act now. Know the difference before the siren sounds.

quiz.question: What is the difference between a weather watch and a warning?
options: A watch means conditions are possible; a warning means it's happening
A watch is more severe than a warning
